[[sn:commons:Category:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja]][[ja:雲見浅間神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660082]] Wikidata name: Kumomi Sengen Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=2018-08-30}} {{Page number|date=2018-08-30}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Kumomi Sengen Shrine | image=[[File:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Honden).jpg|250px|Main Hall of Kumomi Sengen Shrine (Photographed on February 11, 2017)]]
Main Hall of Kumomi Sengen Shrine
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|43|31.7|N|138|44|24.5|E}}}} | location=[[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kamo District, Shizuoka|Kamo District]][[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki Town]] Kumomi 386-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 43 | Latitude_sec = 31.7 | N/S = N | Longitude = 138 | Longitude_min = 44 | Longitude_sec = 24.5 | E/W = E | ISO = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label Position = left | deity=[[Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank=[[Gōsha]], (Traditionally) {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Established=Unknown | Annual Festival=[[July 14]], [[July 15]], [[July 16]] }} '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine''' (雲見浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}}, with an elevation of 162 meters, in {{ill|Kumomi Onsen|ja|雲見温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumomi Onsen|1=Kumomi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660083}}, [[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is also known as '''Kumomi Shrine''' (雲見神社) or '''Ontake Sengen Shrine''' (御嶽浅間宮). It is one of the rare [[Sengen shrine|Asama Shrines]] dedicated solely to {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == Mount Eboshi, also called Ontake (御嶽山) or Kumomi-ga-take (雲見ヶ嶽), is a stand-alone [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] rock formation located at the westernmost coast of the Izu Peninsula. The shrine's main hall is located just below the summit, while the worship hall and the middle shrine (Nakanomiya) are on the mountain's slopes. Historically, the shrine was off-limits to women, who were only allowed to worship at the middle shrine. From the base at [[:en:Japan National Route 136|National Route 136 (Japan)]], the shrine is accessible by a steep 25-minute climb, passing two worship halls before reaching the main shrine. The 428-step stone stairway is extremely steep and includes sections with unstable footing. Due to strong winds near the summit, visitors require sturdy footwear, and light clothing such as high heels is strongly discouraged. Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Honden s3).jpg|Honden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Haiden s2).jpg|Haiden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Nakanomiya).jpg|Nakanomiya Meotomatsu Kumomi-Sengen-jinja.jpg|Sacred [[:en:pine tree|pine tree]] known as "Meoto Matsu" == History == The founding date and origins are unknown. It is traditionally identified with "Ishito no Myojin," listed as "Conferred Fourth Rank, Upper Grade" in the ''Izu Province Shrine Ranking Book,'' and believed to correspond to "Iwanohime no Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō,'' although conclusive evidence is lacking. According to the ''Zushu Shiko,'' the name "Ishito no Myojin" derives from a stone gate called "Asama-mon" in the sea, likely referring to the '''Senganmon''' rock formation about 800 meters south of Mount Eboshi. Shrine lore recounts that the main hall was swept into the sea by a typhoon and rebuilt in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3). The shrine was a center for [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism) and visited by ascetics until the [[Meiji Restoration]] when it was designated as a [[Gōsha]] following the separation of Shinto and Buddhism. After the abolition of the shrine Ranking system post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], local residents have maintained the shrine. == Legends == {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the enshrined deity, is the sister of {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, the deity of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] and the [[Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongŭ Sengen Taisha]], and daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. In mythology, Iwanaga-hime was considered unattractive but promised longevity like a rock to her offspring, while her beautiful sister Konohanasakuya-hime’s children were destined for short lives like flowers. The heavenly grandson {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|瓊瓊杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} rejected Iwanaga-hime, causing her to retreat to Kumomi, leading to enmity between the once-close sisters. According to [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]'s ''Kojikiden,'' the jealous Iwanaga-hime fled to Kumomi, where she was enshrined on a small rocky mountain, while her kind sister stretched tall to look for her, becoming the towering Mount Fuji. Local legends say that when Mount Eboshi is clear, Mount Fuji is shrouded in clouds, and vice versa. Praising Mount Fuji from Mount Eboshi is said to invite misfortune, and "Fuji" was once taboo in local speech. == References == * ''Izu Encyclopedia,'' edited by the Izu Studies Association, Hagoromo Publishing, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} * [[Shrine Ranking system]] * {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}} * [[:en:Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)|Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)]] - Another Asama Shrine dedicated solely to Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto == External Links == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120700015/ Sengen Shrine] - Matsuzaki Town * [http://www.fujisan-jinja.com/shizuoka/kumomi_sengen/ Kumomi Sengen Shrine] - Shrine of Mount Fuji {{Shinto navbox}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|雲見浅間神社|version=103489865|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:雲見浅間神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:History of Matsuzaki]] {{Commons category|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11660082}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja]][[ja:雲見浅間神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660082]] Wikidata name: Kumomi Sengen Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=2018-08-30}} {{Page number|date=2018-08-30}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Kumomi Sengen Shrine | image=[[File:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Honden).jpg|250px|Main Hall of Kumomi Sengen Shrine (Photographed on February 11, 2017)]]
Main Hall of Kumomi Sengen Shrine
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|43|31.7|N|138|44|24.5|E}}}} | location=[[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kamo District, Shizuoka|Kamo District]][[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki Town]] Kumomi 386-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 43 | Latitude_sec = 31.7 | N/S = N | Longitude = 138 | Longitude_min = 44 | Longitude_sec = 24.5 | E/W = E | ISO = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label Position = left | deity=[[Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank=[[Gōsha]], (Traditionally) {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Established=Unknown | Annual Festival=[[July 14]], [[July 15]], [[July 16]] }} '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine''' (雲見浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}}, with an elevation of 162 meters, in {{ill|Kumomi Onsen|ja|雲見温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumomi Onsen|1=Kumomi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660083}}, [[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is also known as '''Kumomi Shrine''' (雲見神社) or '''Ontake Sengen Shrine''' (御嶽浅間宮). It is one of the rare [[Sengen shrine|Asama Shrines]] dedicated solely to {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == Mount Eboshi, also called Ontake (御嶽山) or Kumomi-ga-take (雲見ヶ嶽), is a stand-alone [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] rock formation located at the westernmost coast of the Izu Peninsula. The shrine's main hall is located just below the summit, while the worship hall and the middle shrine (Nakanomiya) are on the mountain's slopes. Historically, the shrine was off-limits to women, who were only allowed to worship at the middle shrine. From the base at [[:en:Japan National Route 136|National Route 136 (Japan)]], the shrine is accessible by a steep 25-minute climb, passing two worship halls before reaching the main shrine. The 428-step stone stairway is extremely steep and includes sections with unstable footing. Due to strong winds near the summit, visitors require sturdy footwear, and light clothing such as high heels is strongly discouraged. Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Honden s3).jpg|Honden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Haiden s2).jpg|Haiden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Nakanomiya).jpg|Nakanomiya Meotomatsu Kumomi-Sengen-jinja.jpg|Sacred [[:en:pine tree|pine tree]] known as "Meoto Matsu" == History == The founding date and origins are unknown. It is traditionally identified with "Ishito no Myojin," listed as "Conferred Fourth Rank, Upper Grade" in the ''Izu Province Shrine Ranking Book,'' and believed to correspond to "Iwanohime no Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō,'' although conclusive evidence is lacking. According to the ''Zushu Shiko,'' the name "Ishito no Myojin" derives from a stone gate called "Asama-mon" in the sea, likely referring to the '''Senganmon''' rock formation about 800 meters south of Mount Eboshi. Shrine lore recounts that the main hall was swept into the sea by a typhoon and rebuilt in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3). The shrine was a center for [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism) and visited by ascetics until the [[Meiji Restoration]] when it was designated as a [[Gōsha]] following the separation of Shinto and Buddhism. After the abolition of the shrine Ranking system post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], local residents have maintained the shrine. == Legends == {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the enshrined deity, is the sister of {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, the deity of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] and the [[Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongŭ Sengen Taisha]], and daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. In mythology, Iwanaga-hime was considered unattractive but promised longevity like a rock to her offspring, while her beautiful sister Konohanasakuya-hime’s children were destined for short lives like flowers. The heavenly grandson {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|瓊瓊杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} rejected Iwanaga-hime, causing her to retreat to Kumomi, leading to enmity between the once-close sisters. According to [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]'s ''Kojikiden,'' the jealous Iwanaga-hime fled to Kumomi, where she was enshrined on a small rocky mountain, while her kind sister stretched tall to look for her, becoming the towering Mount Fuji. Local legends say that when Mount Eboshi is clear, Mount Fuji is shrouded in clouds, and vice versa. Praising Mount Fuji from Mount Eboshi is said to invite misfortune, and "Fuji" was once taboo in local speech. == References == * ''Izu Encyclopedia,'' edited by the Izu Studies Association, Hagoromo Publishing, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} * [[Shrine Ranking system]] * {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}} * [[:en:Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)|Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)]] - Another Asama Shrine dedicated solely to Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto == External Links == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120700015/ Sengen Shrine] - Matsuzaki Town * [http://www.fujisan-jinja.com/shizuoka/kumomi_sengen/ Kumomi Sengen Shrine] - Shrine of Mount Fuji {{Shinto navbox}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|雲見浅間神社|version=103489865|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:雲見浅間神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:History of Matsuzaki]] {{Commons category|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11660082}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

Praising Mount Fuji from Mount Eboshi is said to invite misfortune, and "Fuji" was once taboo in local speech. == References == * ''Izu Encyclopedia,'' edited by the Izu Studies Association, Hagoromo Publishing, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} * [[Shrine Ranking system]] * {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}} * [[:en:Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)|Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)]] - Another Asama Shrine dedicated solely to Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto == External Links == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120700015/ Sengen Shrine] - Matsuzaki Town * [http://www.fujisan-jinja.com/shizuoka/kumomi_sengen/ Kumomi Sengen Shrine] - Shrine of Mount Fuji {{Shinto navbox}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|雲見浅間神社|version=103489865|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:雲見浅間神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:History of Matsuzaki]] {{Commons category|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11660082}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja]][[ja:雲見浅間神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660082]] Wikidata name: Kumomi Sengen Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=2018-08-30}} {{Page number|date=2018-08-30}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Kumomi Sengen Shrine | image=[[File:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Honden).jpg|250px|Main Hall of Kumomi Sengen Shrine (Photographed on February 11, 2017)]]
Main Hall of Kumomi Sengen Shrine
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|43|31.7|N|138|44|24.5|E}}}} | location=[[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kamo District, Shizuoka|Kamo District]][[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki Town]] Kumomi 386-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 43 | Latitude_sec = 31.7 | N/S = N | Longitude = 138 | Longitude_min = 44 | Longitude_sec = 24.5 | E/W = E | ISO = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label Position = left | deity=[[Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank=[[Gōsha]], (Traditionally) {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Established=Unknown | Annual Festival=[[July 14]], [[July 15]], [[July 16]] }} '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine''' (雲見浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}}, with an elevation of 162 meters, in {{ill|Kumomi Onsen|ja|雲見温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumomi Onsen|1=Kumomi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660083}}, [[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is also known as '''Kumomi Shrine''' (雲見神社) or '''Ontake Sengen Shrine''' (御嶽浅間宮). It is one of the rare [[Sengen shrine|Asama Shrines]] dedicated solely to {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == Mount Eboshi, also called Ontake (御嶽山) or Kumomi-ga-take (雲見ヶ嶽), is a stand-alone [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] rock formation located at the westernmost coast of the Izu Peninsula. The shrine's main hall is located just below the summit, while the worship hall and the middle shrine (Nakanomiya) are on the mountain's slopes. Historically, the shrine was off-limits to women, who were only allowed to worship at the middle shrine. From the base at [[:en:Japan National Route 136|National Route 136 (Japan)]], the shrine is accessible by a steep 25-minute climb, passing two worship halls before reaching the main shrine. The 428-step stone stairway is extremely steep and includes sections with unstable footing. Due to strong winds near the summit, visitors require sturdy footwear, and light clothing such as high heels is strongly discouraged. Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Honden s3).jpg|Honden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Haiden s2).jpg|Haiden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Nakanomiya).jpg|Nakanomiya Meotomatsu Kumomi-Sengen-jinja.jpg|Sacred [[:en:pine tree|pine tree]] known as "Meoto Matsu" == History == The founding date and origins are unknown. It is traditionally identified with "Ishito no Myojin," listed as "Conferred Fourth Rank, Upper Grade" in the ''Izu Province Shrine Ranking Book,'' and believed to correspond to "Iwanohime no Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō,'' although conclusive evidence is lacking. According to the ''Zushu Shiko,'' the name "Ishito no Myojin" derives from a stone gate called "Asama-mon" in the sea, likely referring to the '''Senganmon''' rock formation about 800 meters south of Mount Eboshi. Shrine lore recounts that the main hall was swept into the sea by a typhoon and rebuilt in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3). The shrine was a center for [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism) and visited by ascetics until the [[Meiji Restoration]] when it was designated as a [[Gōsha]] following the separation of Shinto and Buddhism. After the abolition of the shrine Ranking system post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], local residents have maintained the shrine. == Legends == {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the enshrined deity, is the sister of {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, the deity of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] and the [[Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongŭ Sengen Taisha]], and daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. In mythology, Iwanaga-hime was considered unattractive but promised longevity like a rock to her offspring, while her beautiful sister Konohanasakuya-hime’s children were destined for short lives like flowers. The heavenly grandson {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|瓊瓊杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} rejected Iwanaga-hime, causing her to retreat to Kumomi, leading to enmity between the once-close sisters. According to [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]'s ''Kojikiden,'' the jealous Iwanaga-hime fled to Kumomi, where she was enshrined on a small rocky mountain, while her kind sister stretched tall to look for her, becoming the towering Mount Fuji. Local legends say that when Mount Eboshi is clear, Mount Fuji is shrouded in clouds, and vice versa. Praising Mount Fuji from Mount Eboshi is said to invite misfortune, and "Fuji" was once taboo in local speech. == References == * ''Izu Encyclopedia,'' edited by the Izu Studies Association, Hagoromo Publishing, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} * [[Shrine Ranking system]] * {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}} * [[:en:Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)|Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)]] - Another Asama Shrine dedicated solely to Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto == External Links == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120700015/ Sengen Shrine] - Matsuzaki Town * [http://www.fujisan-jinja.com/shizuoka/kumomi_sengen/ Kumomi Sengen Shrine] - Shrine of Mount Fuji {{Shinto navbox}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|雲見浅間神社|version=103489865|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:雲見浅間神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:History of Matsuzaki]] {{Commons category|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11660082}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

In mythology, Iwanaga-hime was considered unattractive but promised longevity like a rock to her offspring, while her beautiful sister Konohanasakuya-hime’s children were destined for short lives like flowers. The heavenly grandson {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|瓊瓊杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} rejected Iwanaga-hime, causing her to retreat to Kumomi, leading to enmity between the once-close sisters. According to [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]'s ''Kojikiden,'' the jealous Iwanaga-hime fled to Kumomi, where she was enshrined on a small rocky mountain, while her kind sister stretched tall to look for her, becoming the towering Mount Fuji. Local legends say that when Mount Eboshi is clear, Mount Fuji is shrouded in clouds, and vice versa. Praising Mount Fuji from Mount Eboshi is said to invite misfortune, and "Fuji" was once taboo in local speech. == References == * ''Izu Encyclopedia,'' edited by the Izu Studies Association, Hagoromo Publishing, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} * [[Shrine Ranking system]] * {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}} * [[:en:Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)|Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)]] - Another Asama Shrine dedicated solely to Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto == External Links == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120700015/ Sengen Shrine] - Matsuzaki Town * [http://www.fujisan-jinja.com/shizuoka/kumomi_sengen/ Kumomi Sengen Shrine] - Shrine of Mount Fuji {{Shinto navbox}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|雲見浅間神社|version=103489865|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:雲見浅間神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:History of Matsuzaki]] {{Commons category|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11660082}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine (Q11660082)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja]][[ja:雲見浅間神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660082]] Wikidata name: Kumomi Sengen Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=2018-08-30}} {{Page number|date=2018-08-30}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Kumomi Sengen Shrine | image=[[File:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Honden).jpg|250px|Main Hall of Kumomi Sengen Shrine (Photographed on February 11, 2017)]]
Main Hall of Kumomi Sengen Shrine
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|43|31.7|N|138|44|24.5|E}}}} | location=[[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kamo District, Shizuoka|Kamo District]][[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki Town]] Kumomi 386-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 43 | Latitude_sec = 31.7 | N/S = N | Longitude = 138 | Longitude_min = 44 | Longitude_sec = 24.5 | E/W = E | ISO = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label Position = left | deity=[[Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank=[[Gōsha]], (Traditionally) {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Established=Unknown | Annual Festival=[[July 14]], [[July 15]], [[July 16]] }} '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine''' (雲見浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}}, with an elevation of 162 meters, in {{ill|Kumomi Onsen|ja|雲見温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumomi Onsen|1=Kumomi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660083}}, [[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is also known as '''Kumomi Shrine''' (雲見神社) or '''Ontake Sengen Shrine''' (御嶽浅間宮). It is one of the rare [[Sengen shrine|Asama Shrines]] dedicated solely to {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == Mount Eboshi, also called Ontake (御嶽山) or Kumomi-ga-take (雲見ヶ嶽), is a stand-alone [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] rock formation located at the westernmost coast of the Izu Peninsula. The shrine's main hall is located just below the summit, while the worship hall and the middle shrine (Nakanomiya) are on the mountain's slopes. Historically, the shrine was off-limits to women, who were only allowed to worship at the middle shrine. From the base at [[:en:Japan National Route 136|National Route 136 (Japan)]], the shrine is accessible by a steep 25-minute climb, passing two worship halls before reaching the main shrine. The 428-step stone stairway is extremely steep and includes sections with unstable footing. Due to strong winds near the summit, visitors require sturdy footwear, and light clothing such as high heels is strongly discouraged. Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Honden s3).jpg|Honden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Haiden s2).jpg|Haiden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Nakanomiya).jpg|Nakanomiya Meotomatsu Kumomi-Sengen-jinja.jpg|Sacred [[:en:pine tree|pine tree]] known as "Meoto Matsu" == History == The founding date and origins are unknown. It is traditionally identified with "Ishito no Myojin," listed as "Conferred Fourth Rank, Upper Grade" in the ''Izu Province Shrine Ranking Book,'' and believed to correspond to "Iwanohime no Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō,'' although conclusive evidence is lacking. According to the ''Zushu Shiko,'' the name "Ishito no Myojin" derives from a stone gate called "Asama-mon" in the sea, likely referring to the '''Senganmon''' rock formation about 800 meters south of Mount Eboshi. Shrine lore recounts that the main hall was swept into the sea by a typhoon and rebuilt in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3). The shrine was a center for [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism) and visited by ascetics until the [[Meiji Restoration]] when it was designated as a [[Gōsha]] following the separation of Shinto and Buddhism. After the abolition of the shrine Ranking system post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], local residents have maintained the shrine. == Legends == {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the enshrined deity, is the sister of {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, the deity of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] and the [[Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongŭ Sengen Taisha]], and daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. In mythology, Iwanaga-hime was considered unattractive but promised longevity like a rock to her offspring, while her beautiful sister Konohanasakuya-hime’s children were destined for short lives like flowers. The heavenly grandson {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|瓊瓊杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} rejected Iwanaga-hime, causing her to retreat to Kumomi, leading to enmity between the once-close sisters. According to [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]'s ''Kojikiden,'' the jealous Iwanaga-hime fled to Kumomi, where she was enshrined on a small rocky mountain, while her kind sister stretched tall to look for her, becoming the towering Mount Fuji. Local legends say that when Mount Eboshi is clear, Mount Fuji is shrouded in clouds, and vice versa. Praising Mount Fuji from Mount Eboshi is said to invite misfortune, and "Fuji" was once taboo in local speech. == References == * ''Izu Encyclopedia,'' edited by the Izu Studies Association, Hagoromo Publishing, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} * [[Shrine Ranking system]] * {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}} * [[:en:Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)|Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)]] - Another Asama Shrine dedicated solely to Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto == External Links == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120700015/ Sengen Shrine] - Matsuzaki Town * [http://www.fujisan-jinja.com/shizuoka/kumomi_sengen/ Kumomi Sengen Shrine] - Shrine of Mount Fuji {{Shinto navbox}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|雲見浅間神社|version=103489865|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:雲見浅間神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:History of Matsuzaki]] {{Commons category|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11660082}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine (Q11660082)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Kumomi Sengen Shrine|lt=Kumomi Sengen Shrine|ja|雲見浅間神社|commons|Category:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja|WD=Q11660082}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || — || {{ill|Ihanohimeno- Shrine|lt=Ihanohimeno- Shrine|WD=Q135039653}} || {{coord|34.725472|138.740139|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181593 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11660082}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]]

The shrine's main hall is located just below the summit, while the worship hall and the middle shrine (Nakanomiya) are on the mountain's slopes. From the base at [[:en:Japan National Route 136|National Route 136 (Japan)]], the shrine is accessible by a steep 25-minute climb, passing two worship halls before reaching the main shrine. The 428-step stone stairway is extremely steep and includes sections with unstable footing. Due to strong winds near the summit, visitors require sturdy footwear, and light clothing such as high heels is strongly discouraged. Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Honden s3).jpg|Honden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Haiden s2).jpg|Haiden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Nakanomiya).jpg|Nakanomiya Meotomatsu Kumomi-Sengen-jinja.jpg|Sacred [[:en:pine tree|pine tree]] known as "Meoto Matsu" == History == The founding date and origins are unknown. It is traditionally identified with "Ishito no Myojin," listed as "Conferred Fourth Rank, Upper Grade" in the ''Izu Province Shrine Ranking Book,'' and believed to correspond to "Iwanohime no Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō,'' although conclusive evidence is lacking. According to the ''Zushu Shiko,'' the name "Ishito no Myojin" derives from a stone gate called "Asama-mon" in the sea, likely referring to the '''Senganmon''' rock formation about 800 meters south of Mount Eboshi. Shrine lore recounts that the main hall was swept into the sea by a typhoon and rebuilt in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3). The shrine was a center for [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism) and visited by ascetics until the [[Meiji Restoration]] when it was designated as a [[Gōsha]] following the separation of Shinto and Buddhism. After the abolition of the shrine Ranking system post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], local residents have maintained the shrine. == Legends == {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the enshrined deity, is the sister of {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, the deity of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] and the [[Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongŭ Sengen Taisha]], and daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. In mythology, Iwanaga-hime was considered unattractive but promised longevity like a rock to her offspring, while her beautiful sister Konohanasakuya-hime’s children were destined for short lives like flowers. The heavenly grandson {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|瓊瓊杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} rejected Iwanaga-hime, causing her to retreat to Kumomi, leading to enmity between the once-close sisters. According to [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]'s ''Kojikiden,'' the jealous Iwanaga-hime fled to Kumomi, where she was enshrined on a small rocky mountain, while her kind sister stretched tall to look for her, becoming the towering Mount Fuji. Local legends say that when Mount Eboshi is clear, Mount Fuji is shrouded in clouds, and vice versa. Praising Mount Fuji from Mount Eboshi is said to invite misfortune, and "Fuji" was once taboo in local speech. == References == * ''Izu Encyclopedia,'' edited by the Izu Studies Association, Hagoromo Publishing, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} * [[Shrine Ranking system]] * {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}} * [[:en:Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)|Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)]] - Another Asama Shrine dedicated solely to Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto == External Links == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120700015/ Sengen Shrine] - Matsuzaki Town * [http://www.fujisan-jinja.com/shizuoka/kumomi_sengen/ Kumomi Sengen Shrine] - Shrine of Mount Fuji {{Shinto navbox}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|雲見浅間神社|version=103489865|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:雲見浅間神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:History of Matsuzaki]] {{Commons category|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine (Q11660082)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! The 428-step stone stairway is extremely steep and includes sections with unstable footing. Due to strong winds near the summit, visitors require sturdy footwear, and light clothing such as high heels is strongly discouraged. Main Hall of Kumomi Sengen Shrine
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|43|31.7|N|138|44|24.5|E}}}} | location=[[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kamo District, Shizuoka|Kamo District]][[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki Town]] Kumomi 386-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 43 | Latitude_sec = 31.7 | N/S = N | Longitude = 138 | Longitude_min = 44 | Longitude_sec = 24.5 | E/W = E | ISO = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label Position = left | deity=[[Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank=[[Gōsha]], (Traditionally) {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Established=Unknown | Annual Festival=[[July 14]], [[July 15]], [[July 16]] }} '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine''' (雲見浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}}, with an elevation of 162 meters, in {{ill|Kumomi Onsen|ja|雲見温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumomi Onsen|1=Kumomi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660083}}, [[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is also known as '''Kumomi Shrine''' (雲見神社) or '''Ontake Sengen Shrine''' (御嶽浅間宮). It is one of the rare [[Sengen shrine|Asama Shrines]] dedicated solely to {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == Mount Eboshi, also called Ontake (御嶽山) or Kumomi-ga-take (雲見ヶ嶽), is a stand-alone [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] rock formation located at the westernmost coast of the Izu Peninsula. The shrine's main hall is located just below the summit, while the worship hall and the middle shrine (Nakanomiya) are on the mountain's slopes. Historically, the shrine was off-limits to women, who were only allowed to worship at the middle shrine. From the base at [[:en:Japan National Route 136|National Route 136 (Japan)]], the shrine is accessible by a steep 25-minute climb, passing two worship halls before reaching the main shrine. The 428-step stone stairway is extremely steep and includes sections with unstable footing. Due to strong winds near the summit, visitors require sturdy footwear, and light clothing such as high heels is strongly discouraged. Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Honden s3).jpg|Honden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Haiden s2).jpg|Haiden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Nakanomiya).jpg|Nakanomiya Meotomatsu Kumomi-Sengen-jinja.jpg|Sacred [[:en:pine tree|pine tree]] known as "Meoto Matsu" == History == The founding date and origins are unknown. It is traditionally identified with "Ishito no Myojin," listed as "Conferred Fourth Rank, Upper Grade" in the ''Izu Province Shrine Ranking Book,'' and believed to correspond to "Iwanohime no Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō,'' although conclusive evidence is lacking. According to the ''Zushu Shiko,'' the name "Ishito no Myojin" derives from a stone gate called "Asama-mon" in the sea, likely referring to the '''Senganmon''' rock formation about 800 meters south of Mount Eboshi. Shrine lore recounts that the main hall was swept into the sea by a typhoon and rebuilt in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3). The shrine was a center for [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism) and visited by ascetics until the [[Meiji Restoration]] when it was designated as a [[Gōsha]] following the separation of Shinto and Buddhism. After the abolition of the shrine Ranking system post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], local residents have maintained the shrine. == Legends == {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the enshrined deity, is the sister of {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, the deity of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] and the [[Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongŭ Sengen Taisha]], and daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. In mythology, Iwanaga-hime was considered unattractive but promised longevity like a rock to her offspring, while her beautiful sister Konohanasakuya-hime’s children were destined for short lives like flowers. The heavenly grandson {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|瓊瓊杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} rejected Iwanaga-hime, causing her to retreat to Kumomi, leading to enmity between the once-close sisters. According to [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]'s ''Kojikiden,'' the jealous Iwanaga-hime fled to Kumomi, where she was enshrined on a small rocky mountain, while her kind sister stretched tall to look for her, becoming the towering Mount Fuji. Local legends say that when Mount Eboshi is clear, Mount Fuji is shrouded in clouds, and vice versa. Praising Mount Fuji from Mount Eboshi is said to invite misfortune, and "Fuji" was once taboo in local speech. == References == * ''Izu Encyclopedia,'' edited by the Izu Studies Association, Hagoromo Publishing, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} * [[Shrine Ranking system]] * {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}} * [[:en:Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)|Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)]] - Another Asama Shrine dedicated solely to Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto == External Links == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120700015/ Sengen Shrine] - Matsuzaki Town * [http://www.fujisan-jinja.com/shizuoka/kumomi_sengen/ Kumomi Sengen Shrine] - Shrine of Mount Fuji {{Shinto navbox}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|雲見浅間神社|version=103489865|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:雲見浅間神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:History of Matsuzaki]] {{Commons category|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine (Q11660082)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. Praising Mount Fuji from Mount Eboshi is said to invite misfortune, and "Fuji" was once taboo in local speech. == References == * ''Izu Encyclopedia,'' edited by the Izu Studies Association, Hagoromo Publishing, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} * [[Shrine Ranking system]] * {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}} * [[:en:Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)|Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)]] - Another Asama Shrine dedicated solely to Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto == External Links == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120700015/ Sengen Shrine] - Matsuzaki Town * [http://www.fujisan-jinja.com/shizuoka/kumomi_sengen/ Kumomi Sengen Shrine] - Shrine of Mount Fuji {{Shinto navbox}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|雲見浅間神社|version=103489865|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:雲見浅間神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:History of Matsuzaki]] {{Commons category|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine (Q11660082)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Kumomi Sengen Shrine|lt=Kumomi Sengen Shrine|ja|雲見浅間神社|commons|Category:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja|WD=Q11660082}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || — || {{ill|Ihanohimeno- Shrine|lt=Ihanohimeno- Shrine|WD=Q135039653}} || {{coord|34.725472|138.740139|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181593 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] {{wikidata link|Q11660082}}==Notes==

[[commons:Category:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja]][[ja:雲見浅間神社]] [[commons:Category:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja]][[ja:雲見浅間神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja]][[ja:雲見浅間神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660082]] Wikidata name: Kumomi Sengen Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=2018-08-30}} {{Page number|date=2018-08-30}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Kumomi Sengen Shrine | image=[[File:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Honden).jpg|250px|Main Hall of Kumomi Sengen Shrine (Photographed on February 11, 2017)]]
Main Hall of Kumomi Sengen Shrine
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|43|31.7|N|138|44|24.5|E}}}} | location=[[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kamo District, Shizuoka|Kamo District]][[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki Town]] Kumomi 386-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 43 | Latitude_sec = 31.7 | N/S = N | Longitude = 138 | Longitude_min = 44 | Longitude_sec = 24.5 | E/W = E | ISO = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label Position = left | deity=[[Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank=[[Gōsha]], (Traditionally) {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Established=Unknown | Annual Festival=[[July 14]], [[July 15]], [[July 16]] }} '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine''' (雲見浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}}, with an elevation of 162 meters, in {{ill|Kumomi Onsen|ja|雲見温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumomi Onsen|1=Kumomi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660083}}, [[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is also known as '''Kumomi Shrine''' (雲見神社) or '''Ontake Sengen Shrine''' (御嶽浅間宮). It is one of the rare [[Sengen shrine|Asama Shrines]] dedicated solely to {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == Mount Eboshi, also called Ontake (御嶽山) or Kumomi-ga-take (雲見ヶ嶽), is a stand-alone [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] rock formation located at the westernmost coast of the Izu Peninsula. The shrine's main hall is located just below the summit, while the worship hall and the middle shrine (Nakanomiya) are on the mountain's slopes. Historically, the shrine was off-limits to women, who were only allowed to worship at the middle shrine. From the base at [[:en:Japan National Route 136|National Route 136 (Japan)]], the shrine is accessible by a steep 25-minute climb, passing two worship halls before reaching the main shrine. The 428-step stone stairway is extremely steep and includes sections with unstable footing. Due to strong winds near the summit, visitors require sturdy footwear, and light clothing such as high heels is strongly discouraged. Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Honden s3).jpg|Honden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Haiden s2).jpg|Haiden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Nakanomiya).jpg|Nakanomiya Meotomatsu Kumomi-Sengen-jinja.jpg|Sacred [[:en:pine tree|pine tree]] known as "Meoto Matsu" == History == The founding date and origins are unknown. It is traditionally identified with "Ishito no Myojin," listed as "Conferred Fourth Rank, Upper Grade" in the ''Izu Province Shrine Ranking Book,'' and believed to correspond to "Iwanohime no Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō,'' although conclusive evidence is lacking. According to the ''Zushu Shiko,'' the name "Ishito no Myojin" derives from a stone gate called "Asama-mon" in the sea, likely referring to the '''Senganmon''' rock formation about 800 meters south of Mount Eboshi. Shrine lore recounts that the main hall was swept into the sea by a typhoon and rebuilt in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3). The shrine was a center for [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism) and visited by ascetics until the [[Meiji Restoration]] when it was designated as a [[Gōsha]] following the separation of Shinto and Buddhism. After the abolition of the shrine Ranking system post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], local residents have maintained the shrine. == Legends == {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the enshrined deity, is the sister of {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, the deity of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] and the [[Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongŭ Sengen Taisha]], and daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. In mythology, Iwanaga-hime was considered unattractive but promised longevity like a rock to her offspring, while her beautiful sister Konohanasakuya-hime’s children were destined for short lives like flowers. The heavenly grandson {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|瓊瓊杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} rejected Iwanaga-hime, causing her to retreat to Kumomi, leading to enmity between the once-close sisters. According to [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]'s ''Kojikiden,'' the jealous Iwanaga-hime fled to Kumomi, where she was enshrined on a small rocky mountain, while her kind sister stretched tall to look for her, becoming the towering Mount Fuji. Local legends say that when Mount Eboshi is clear, Mount Fuji is shrouded in clouds, and vice versa. Praising Mount Fuji from Mount Eboshi is said to invite misfortune, and "Fuji" was once taboo in local speech. == References == * ''Izu Encyclopedia,'' edited by the Izu Studies Association, Hagoromo Publishing, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} * [[Shrine Ranking system]] * {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}} * [[:en:Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)|Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)]] - Another Asama Shrine dedicated solely to Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto == External Links == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120700015/ Sengen Shrine] - Matsuzaki Town * [http://www.fujisan-jinja.com/shizuoka/kumomi_sengen/ Kumomi Sengen Shrine] - Shrine of Mount Fuji {{Shinto navbox}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|雲見浅間神社|version=103489865|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:雲見浅間神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:History of Matsuzaki]] {{Commons category|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine (Q11660082)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Kumomi Sengen Shrine|lt=Kumomi Sengen Shrine|ja|雲見浅間神社|commons|Category:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja|WD=Q11660082}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || — || {{ill|Ihanohimeno- Shrine|lt=Ihanohimeno- Shrine|WD=Q135039653}} || {{coord|34.725472|138.740139|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181593 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] {{wikidata link|Q11660082}}==Notes==

Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Honden s3).jpg|Honden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Haiden s2).jpg|Haiden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Nakanomiya).jpg|Nakanomiya Meotomatsu Kumomi-Sengen-jinja.jpg|Sacred [[:en:pine tree|pine tree]] known as "Meoto Matsu" == History == The founding date and origins are unknown. It is traditionally identified with "Ishito no Myojin," listed as "Conferred Fourth Rank, Upper Grade" in the ''Izu Province Shrine Ranking Book,'' and believed to correspond to "Iwanohime no Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō,'' although conclusive evidence is lacking. According to the ''Zushu Shiko,'' the name "Ishito no Myojin" derives from a stone gate called "Asama-mon" in the sea, likely referring to the '''Senganmon''' rock formation about 800 meters south of Mount Eboshi. Shrine lore recounts that the main hall was swept into the sea by a typhoon and rebuilt in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3). The shrine was a center for [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism) and visited by ascetics until the [[Meiji Restoration]] when it was designated as a [[Gōsha]] following the separation of Shinto and Buddhism. After the abolition of the shrine Ranking system post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], local residents have maintained the shrine. == Legends == {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the enshrined deity, is the sister of {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, the deity of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] and the [[Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongŭ Sengen Taisha]], and daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. In mythology, Iwanaga-hime was considered unattractive but promised longevity like a rock to her offspring, while her beautiful sister Konohanasakuya-hime’s children were destined for short lives like flowers. The heavenly grandson {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|瓊瓊杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} rejected Iwanaga-hime, causing her to retreat to Kumomi, leading to enmity between the once-close sisters. According to [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]'s ''Kojikiden,'' the jealous Iwanaga-hime fled to Kumomi, where she was enshrined on a small rocky mountain, while her kind sister stretched tall to look for her, becoming the towering Mount Fuji. Local legends say that when Mount Eboshi is clear, Mount Fuji is shrouded in clouds, and vice versa. Praising Mount Fuji from Mount Eboshi is said to invite misfortune, and "Fuji" was once taboo in local speech. == References == * ''Izu Encyclopedia,'' edited by the Izu Studies Association, Hagoromo Publishing, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} * [[Shrine Ranking system]] * {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}} * [[:en:Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)|Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)]] - Another Asama Shrine dedicated solely to Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto == External Links == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120700015/ Sengen Shrine] - Matsuzaki Town * [http://www.fujisan-jinja.com/shizuoka/kumomi_sengen/ Kumomi Sengen Shrine] - Shrine of Mount Fuji {{Shinto navbox}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|雲見浅間神社|version=103489865|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:雲見浅間神社]] {{Commons category|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine (Q11660082)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|43|31.7|N|138|44|24.5|E}}}} | location=[[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kamo District, Shizuoka|Kamo District]][[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki Town]] Kumomi 386-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 43 | Latitude_sec = 31.7 | N/S = N | Longitude = 138 | Longitude_min = 44 | Longitude_sec = 24.5 | E/W = E | ISO = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label Position = left | deity=[[Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank=[[Gōsha]], (Traditionally) {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Established=Unknown | Annual Festival=[[July 14]], [[July 15]], [[July 16]] }} '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine''' (雲見浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}}, with an elevation of 162 meters, in {{ill|Kumomi Onsen|ja|雲見温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumomi Onsen|1=Kumomi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660083}}, [[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is also known as '''Kumomi Shrine''' (雲見神社) or '''Ontake Sengen Shrine''' (御嶽浅間宮). It is one of the rare [[Sengen shrine|Asama Shrines]] dedicated solely to {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == Mount Eboshi, also called Ontake (御嶽山) or Kumomi-ga-take (雲見ヶ嶽), is a stand-alone [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] rock formation located at the westernmost coast of the Izu Peninsula. The shrine's main hall is located just below the summit, while the worship hall and the middle shrine (Nakanomiya) are on the mountain's slopes. Historically, the shrine was off-limits to women, who were only allowed to worship at the middle shrine. From the base at [[:en:Japan National Route 136|National Route 136 (Japan)]], the shrine is accessible by a steep 25-minute climb, passing two worship halls before reaching the main shrine. The 428-step stone stairway is extremely steep and includes sections with unstable footing. Due to strong winds near the summit, visitors require sturdy footwear, and light clothing such as high heels is strongly discouraged. [[commons:Category:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja]][[ja:雲見浅間神社]] [[commons:Category:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja]][[ja:雲見浅間神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja]][[ja:雲見浅間神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660082]] Wikidata name: Kumomi Sengen Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=2018-08-30}} {{Page number|date=2018-08-30}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Kumomi Sengen Shrine | image=[[File:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Honden).jpg|250px|Main Hall of Kumomi Sengen Shrine (Photographed on February 11, 2017)]]
Main Hall of Kumomi Sengen Shrine
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|43|31.7|N|138|44|24.5|E}}}} | location=[[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kamo District, Shizuoka|Kamo District]][[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki Town]] Kumomi 386-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 43 | Latitude_sec = 31.7 | N/S = N | Longitude = 138 | Longitude_min = 44 | Longitude_sec = 24.5 | E/W = E | ISO = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label Position = left | deity=[[Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank=[[Gōsha]], (Traditionally) {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Established=Unknown | Annual Festival=[[July 14]], [[July 15]], [[July 16]] }} '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine''' (雲見浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}}, with an elevation of 162 meters, in {{ill|Kumomi Onsen|ja|雲見温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumomi Onsen|1=Kumomi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660083}}, [[:en:Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is also known as '''Kumomi Shrine''' (雲見神社) or '''Ontake Sengen Shrine''' (御嶽浅間宮). It is one of the rare [[Sengen shrine|Asama Shrines]] dedicated solely to {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == Mount Eboshi, also called Ontake (御嶽山) or Kumomi-ga-take (雲見ヶ嶽), is a stand-alone [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] rock formation located at the westernmost coast of the Izu Peninsula. The shrine's main hall is located just below the summit, while the worship hall and the middle shrine (Nakanomiya) are on the mountain's slopes. Historically, the shrine was off-limits to women, who were only allowed to worship at the middle shrine. From the base at [[:en:Japan National Route 136|National Route 136 (Japan)]], the shrine is accessible by a steep 25-minute climb, passing two worship halls before reaching the main shrine. The 428-step stone stairway is extremely steep and includes sections with unstable footing. Due to strong winds near the summit, visitors require sturdy footwear, and light clothing such as high heels is strongly discouraged. Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Honden s3).jpg|Honden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Haiden s2).jpg|Haiden Kumomi-Sengen-jinja (Nakanomiya).jpg|Nakanomiya Meotomatsu Kumomi-Sengen-jinja.jpg|Sacred [[:en:pine tree|pine tree]] known as "Meoto Matsu" == History == The founding date and origins are unknown. It is traditionally identified with "Ishito no Myojin," listed as "Conferred Fourth Rank, Upper Grade" in the ''Izu Province Shrine Ranking Book,'' and believed to correspond to "Iwanohime no Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō,'' although conclusive evidence is lacking. According to the ''Zushu Shiko,'' the name "Ishito no Myojin" derives from a stone gate called "Asama-mon" in the sea, likely referring to the '''Senganmon''' rock formation about 800 meters south of Mount Eboshi. Shrine lore recounts that the main hall was swept into the sea by a typhoon and rebuilt in [[:en:1657|1657]] (Meireki 3). The shrine was a center for [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism) and visited by ascetics until the [[Meiji Restoration]] when it was designated as a [[Gōsha]] following the separation of Shinto and Buddhism. After the abolition of the shrine Ranking system post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], local residents have maintained the shrine. == Legends == {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|ja|磐長姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the enshrined deity, is the sister of {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, the deity of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] and the [[Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Fujisan Hongŭ Sengen Taisha]], and daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. In mythology, Iwanaga-hime was considered unattractive but promised longevity like a rock to her offspring, while her beautiful sister Konohanasakuya-hime’s children were destined for short lives like flowers. The heavenly grandson {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|瓊瓊杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} rejected Iwanaga-hime, causing her to retreat to Kumomi, leading to enmity between the once-close sisters. According to [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]'s ''Kojikiden,'' the jealous Iwanaga-hime fled to Kumomi, where she was enshrined on a small rocky mountain, while her kind sister stretched tall to look for her, becoming the towering Mount Fuji. Local legends say that when Mount Eboshi is clear, Mount Fuji is shrouded in clouds, and vice versa. Praising Mount Fuji from Mount Eboshi is said to invite misfortune, and "Fuji" was once taboo in local speech. == References == * ''Izu Encyclopedia,'' edited by the Izu Studies Association, Hagoromo Publishing, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} * [[Shrine Ranking system]] * {{ill|Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|ja|烏帽子山 (松崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Eboshi (Matsuzaki, Shizuoka)|1=Mount Eboshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47461946}} * [[:en:Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)|Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)]] - Another Asama Shrine dedicated solely to Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto == External Links == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120700015/ Sengen Shrine] - Matsuzaki Town * [http://www.fujisan-jinja.com/shizuoka/kumomi_sengen/ Kumomi Sengen Shrine] - Shrine of Mount Fuji {{Shinto navbox}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|雲見浅間神社|version=103489865|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雲見浅間神社]] {{Commons category|Kumomi-Sengen-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kumomi Sengen Shrine (Q11660082)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Kumomi Sengen Shrine|lt=Kumomi Sengen Shrine|ja|雲見浅間神社|commons|Category:Kumomi-Sengen-jinja|WD=Q11660082}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || — || — || {{ill|Ihanohimeno- Shrine|lt=Ihanohimeno- Shrine|WD=Q135039653}} || {{coord|34.725472|138.740139|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181593 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11660082}}==Notes== [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Matsuzaki]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
